resume_instructions: Read the objective, backlog, and work-state files before editing code.
Inspect `.pack-state/agent-memory/latest-memory.json` first when it exists, then fall back to other `.pack-state/agent-memory/*.json` files.
Start the next cycle from the selected Support Cockpit direction rather than the older reviewed webpage frame.
Preserve the sticky quick-jump behavior because that is the operator-confirmed reason Support Cockpit won the prototype comparison.
Use the cleaned Support Cockpit chassis as the next container for a real playbook iteration instead of reintroducing the older boxed legacy page structures.
The command-first flow strip and the horizontal dependency path now sit above the main two-column layout, and Quick jump starts level with the first stepwise checkpoint content.
The current active content task is reviewing and tightening the rebuilt ASMS UI is down page, which now uses a from-scratch system-thinking layout instead of the older service-first page shape.
The live ASMS UI page now uses five useful-work checkpoints: host support, Apache/HTTPD serving the UI, auth branch, app branch, and useful-work stop point.
Keep treating the current ASMS UI page as a first real system-thinking reference playbook and tighten it through review rather than reverting to the older incremental patch model.

The current ASMS UI service pack now uses real Keycloak readiness and OIDC path checks, a real ms-metro heartbeat check, and file-based Keycloak and ms-metro logs taken from the lab-backed appliance behavior.
Treat ActiveMQ as a supporting dependency for the ASMS UI path, not a first-pass UI-down check, unless ms-metro logs or behavior point to JMS or broker problems.

The current evidence-backed ASMS UI working model is host health -> Apache edge -> legacy post-shell checks -> BusinessFlow checkpoint -> `BusinessFlow -> AFA connection` or `BusinessFlow -> AFF connection` when deep health names them -> later Keycloak and FireFlow auth handoff -> `/afa/php/home.php` render -> supporting post-home home-shell clues and light Metro refresh -> broader app traffic.
The systems-thinking method is now proven on the lab: reproduce one bounded browser flow, correlate the same minute in Apache and downstream logs, and only then update the playbook model.
Keep observed and inferred dependency claims separate. The value comes from reducing support effort with real path evidence, not from forcing a cleaner serial chain than the lab actually shows.

Treat disk, memory, OOM, and other core host-resource checks as the shared Host Health Pre-Check pack defined in docs/specs/adf-host-health-precheck-pack-v1.md and use it at the start of every major playbook.
Keep the stronger Keycloak and ms-metro command coverage, but reframe those checks as auth-branch and app-branch useful-work checks in the rebuild.

Continue following the customer-visible path through BusinessFlow and FireFlow because BusinessFlow is now the first named operational checkpoint in the observed login path and FireFlow is now a later auth-coupled module that can change the engineer's next move.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-backward-dependency-trajectory-note-20260326.md` as the current trajectory note: keep the core `ASMS UI is down` path bounded, then walk backward through BusinessFlow first, FireFlow second, and only then through supporting dependencies.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-fireflow-dependency-seams-pass-20260326.md` as the current staged seam note: BusinessFlow's closest readable neighbors are Postgres, AFA, and AFF, while FireFlow's closest readable neighbors are Apache proxying, aff-boot, and database-backed runtime behavior.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-health-implementation-pass-20260326.md` as the current BusinessFlow deep-health note: the `AFA connection` and `AFF connection` checks are local HTTPS session-liveness checks rather than raw direct 8080 or 1989 probes.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-local-https-ownership-pass-20260326.md` as the local-HTTPS ownership note: the BusinessFlow AFF side is now reduced to Apache `443` -> `aff-boot` `1989`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-afa-local-ownership-pass-20260326.md` as the AFA local-ownership note: `/afa/php/...` is Apache-fronted local PHP, while `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` are Metro-backed proxy families.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-afa-soap-path-pass-20260326.md` as the final AFA path note: the BusinessFlow `AFA connection` deep-health check lands on `https://localhost/afa/php/ws.php` and uses SOAP `isSessionAlive`, not `/afa/php/commands.php`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-afa-soap-runtime-correlation-pass-20260326.md` as the runtime seam note: Apache only gives timing for `ws.php`, while `.ht-fa-history` provides the request hash, `SESSION / TOKEN / COOKIE` triple, and `is_session_alive` result, and the next direct seam is the PHP session file plus `.ht-LastActionTime` for that same session id.

Treat the immediate backend seams behind `ms-bflow.service` on `8081` and `aff-boot.service` on `1989` as the next support-relevant dependency surfaces to map, because those are the module checkpoints most likely to make a case look like `ASMS UI is down` before it becomes a narrower subsystem issue.

Keep ActiveMQ behind those module checkpoints for now. It is now a proven later supporting dependency for the FireFlow seam because aff-boot holds live broker connections on 61616, but the current lab evidence still keeps it behind the closer route, service, and database checks instead of treating it as a first-pass `GUI down` gate.

Within the BusinessFlow seam, keep the new deep-health implementation result in view: `AFA connection` and `AFF connection` are local HTTPS session-liveness checks, so the immediate seam stays closer to Apache and those app routes than to raw 8080, 1989, or broker theory.

Treat the BusinessFlow seam as reduced enough to name directly in the current playbook: `AFA connection` lands on Apache-served local PHP SOAP `/afa/php/ws.php`, and `AFF connection` lands on Apache `443` -> `aff-boot` `1989` via `/FireFlow/api/session`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-main-stop-logic-refinement-pass-20260326.md` as the top-level stop-logic note: Step 3 now stops at named BusinessFlow child seams, Step 4 stays bounded to first usable home-shell plus immediate hydration, and Step 5 is for naming the first clear stop point or branching out of `GUI down`.

For the AFA side, stop the next support step at `.ht-fa-history` and the PHP session backing files before jumping outward to Metro. The first useful follow-on seam is `ws.php` -> `.ht-fa-history` -> `/var/lib/php/session/sess_<id>` -> `.ht-LastActionTime`.

Within the FireFlow seam, keep Apache proxying, aff-boot, and database-backed runtime evidence ahead of ActiveMQ for first-pass troubleshooting. Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-fireflow-broker-dependency-pass-20260326.md` plus `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-fireflow-broker-correlation-pass-20260326.md` as the supporting notes: aff-boot holds live broker connections on 61616, but the reproduced ASMS login-handoff minute still centered on auth, session, and REST activity instead of broker-side signals.

Keep the first post-home checks inside the ASMS playbook for now, but demote them to supporting clues: `FireFlowBridge.js`, basic home assets, and nearby Metro routes help explain the same minute after `/afa/php/home.php`, but they are not first-class gates unless the rendered home shell itself degrades.

Within those nearby Metro routes, treat `GET /afa/getStatus` as the strongest immediate supporting clue after the first usable shell appears. Keep `GET /afa/api/v1/license`, `GET /afa/api/v1/config?...`, `GET /afa/api/v1/config/all/noauth?domain=0`, and `POST /afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` as same-minute but still unproven first-shell requirements until a fresh-session isolation pass proves one of them matters.

The latest fresh-session pass now ties `GET /afa/api/v1/config?...` and `POST /afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` more directly to the new `PHPSESSID`, so they should be treated as stronger fresh-session clues than before, but still not as proven first-shell gates.

A later CDP `Network.setBlockedURLs(...)` pass matched only the early `config/PRINT_TABLE_IN_NORMAL_VIEW` probe and did not record real block events for the fresh-session `license`, `config`, `config/all/noauth`, or `session/extend` routes, so browser-side blocking is not yet a reliable control point for those requests on this lab.

The route-ownership pass now shows Apache exposing separate `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` proxy families toward Metro, while the fresh-session minute showed paired `/afa/config/...` and `/afa/api/v1/config?...` surfaces on the server side.

Variant A of the Apache seam mutation has now run. Denying `/afa/external` returned repeated `403` responses for that family but still left `/afa/php/home.php` fully usable, so `/afa/external` is now demoted as a first-shell gate candidate.

Variant B of the Apache seam mutation has also run. Denying the top-level `/afa/api/v1` Location still left `/afa/php/home.php` fully usable, and same-minute Metro logs still showed fresh-session `/afa/api/v1/config?...` and `/afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` as `200`, so the top-level Apache deny is not a clean control lever for that path.

Keep `POST /afa/api/v1/bridge/refresh?...` demoted from the ASMS first-shell gate model for now because the latest lab pass tied it to a different long-lived session instead of the reproduced fresh login session.

The newer route-owner split is now explicit: Apache owns browser-facing `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` proxy families, while PHP `RestClient` also issues direct `http://127.0.0.1:8080/afa/config/...` requests.
Treat the remaining `config` question as browser-owned versus PHP-owned route ownership, not as another family-wide Apache deny candidate.
Prefer one read-only correlation pass that proves which Metro `config` requests have no matching Apache parent before planning another mutation.

That read-only correlation pass is now complete for fresh session `jkp0cnnf971ksgifu3k25jopiq`: browser-facing `/afa/external/...` lines matched Metro `/afa/api/v1/...`, while direct `/afa/config/...` and same-minute `/afa/getStatus` lines had no Apache parent.
Treat the remaining unresolved seam as mostly the internal direct `/afa/config/...` family behind PHP `RestClient`, not as another browser-facing proxy-family question.

The smallest auth-side named read is now already demoted by observation: `GET /afa/config/Case_Sensitive_Username?...` returns `404` on the lab and `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` already falls back without breaking login.
Treat the broader `AlgosecSession::__construct()` / `GetConfigurationSettings()` bundle as the first unresolved internal config surface worth planning next.

The broader bundle should now be approached through login bootstrap, not `home.php`: `home.php` mostly consumes an existing `AlgosecSession`, while `AlgosecSessionManager::executeLogin()` and `postLoginActions()` own the first bootstrap bundle loads.

Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-login-bootstrap-control-path-pass-20260326.md` before planning the next internal mutation so the first app-level experiment starts at the bootstrap hook instead of another coarse transport seam.
Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-login-bootstrap-config-isolation-plan-v1.md` as the closed record of the bootstrap-bundle experiment family.

Treat Variant C1, Variant C2, and Variant C3 as all reduced for first-shell gating: skipping the post-login recache, bypassing the constructor-time bundle, and suppressing both together still left `/afa/php/home.php` usable on the lab.

Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-postlogin-handleconfigparams-variant-c1-pass-20260326.md`, `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-constructor-config-bypass-variant-c2-pass-20260326.md`, and `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-combined-bootstrap-variant-c3-pass-20260326.md` as the canonical bootstrap results.

Shift the next seam work away from bootstrap config-bundle theory and toward later demand-loaded home-shell behavior, especially `dynamic.js.php`, dashboard hydration, the first interactive home-shell actions, and the surviving config-family route ownership after `/afa/php/home.php`.

Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-dashboard-demand-load-pass-20260326.md` as the canonical note for the reduced later-shell picture: the first usable shell hydrates `DISPLAY_ISSUES_CENTER`, `GET_DASHBOARDS_DATA`, `GET_CHART_FOR_DASHBOARD_MULTIPLE`, and `GET_RISK_PROFILES_OPTIONS` before a deeper operator action is isolated.
Treat `DEVICES` as a shell-context and tree-refresh step in the bounded pass, not yet as the first clearly separate backend seam.
Treat `Analyze` as the first clean deeper operator action isolated so far, because it produced `GET_ANALYSIS_OPTIONS` only after the landing shell and device context were already visible.

Carry forward the code-trace result from `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-dashboard-demand-load-pass-20260326.md`: `Analyze` first opens a dialog and fetches options through `SubmitAnalysisHTMLOptionsRequest(...)`, so the next seam is still narrower than a full analysis run.

Carry forward the server-side boundary too: the dialog path uses `GET_ANALYZE_DATA`, while the real `Start Analysis` step posts `cmd=ANALYZE` and launches `RunFaServer(...)`, so that step is already a later workflow branch rather than part of the first usable-shell gate.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-policy-tab-branch-pass-20260326.md` too: the device-context `POLICY` tab already posts `GET_POLICY_TAB` and `GET_DEVICE_POLICY`, prepares `PolicyTabIframe.php`, and can depend on analyzed report data, so it is a later content branch rather than part of the first usable-shell gate.

Treat the sibling device-context tabs the same way unless stronger evidence proves otherwise: `CHANGES` uses `GET_MONITORING_CHANGES` and `REPORTS` uses `GET_REPORTS`, so they also belong to later content branches rather than to the first usable-shell gate.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-gui-up-classification-note-20260326.md` as the operator-facing classification rule: once the customer can navigate the devices tree and open `REPORTS` to view a report, stop classifying the case as `GUI down`; reaching `Analyze` is optional extra confirmation rather than a required boundary.
Use docs/specs/adf-controlled-lab-mutation-experiment-pattern-v1.md as the reusable mutation template for future ADF seam experiments.

Use docs/specs/asms-ui-apache-metro-proxy-isolation-plan-v1.md as the closed record of the family-wide Apache seam test, then continue with docs/specs/asms-ui-config-owner-split-plan-v1.md and docs/specs/asms-ui-direct-config-seam-plan-v1.md.
Treat routes like Notification Center or watchdog-linked issue counts as later subsystem candidates unless the reproduced browser minute proves they are the first real stop point.
After the ASMS UI rebuild proves the system-thinking pattern, deepen the next dependency-aware playbook branches before returning to a broader wording pass.
Use docs/specs/adf-language-standard-v1.md as the wording contract when command coverage work introduces new operator-facing text.
The built-in Starlight `On this page` rail is now removed from the playbook page so the center pane has more horizontal room for command-first content.
The dependency path is now horizontal inside the center pane and still supports click-to-jump into the matching checkpoint details below.

There is also a separate pending planning item to review whether the remote Codex persona and pack-local agent guidance should evolve toward an ADF-specific Linux troubleshooting and diagnostic-authoring profile.
Restage the build-pack to the saved AlgoSec lab target before the next remote Codex run that changes playbook logic.
Read docs/specs/adf-remote-codex-invocation-notes-v1.md before the next remote Codex launch.
For controlled lab seam mutations, follow docs/specs/adf-controlled-lab-mutation-experiment-pattern-v1.md and prefer a temporary Apache override file over editing the base config inline.

For the next ASMS narrowing cycle, start from `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-config-correlation-pass-20260326.md` and docs/specs/asms-ui-direct-config-seam-plan-v1.md instead of another Apache-family or browser-only mutation.

For the current ASMS investigative authoring phase, use PackFactory target prep and staging (`prepare_remote_autonomy_target.py` and `push_build_pack_to_remote.py`) before guided remote Codex work on the staged pack.

Do not use `run_remote_autonomy_loop.py` as the default path for the current ASMS subsystem-investigation cycles. The bounded autonomy wrapper is right for starter-backlog autonomy proofs, but it will correctly stop when the work needs guided source edits or appliance-backed artifact regeneration.
Use a small remote shell script plus env -i and /bin/bash --noprofile --norc for the outer appliance launcher instead of a long inline SSH command.

For ASMS browser-backed investigation, do not wait on Playwright `networkidle` after login. Use bounded waits plus visible-shell checks like title `AlgoSec - Home`, final URL `/afa/php/home.php`, and visible home markers instead.
Clear stale matching codex exec processes before relaunching, and confirm both the result file and rebuilt starlight-site timestamps before assuming the remote run finished cleanly.
Keep machine-readable artifacts canonical, keep HTML as a thin render layer, and keep new commands read-only.
Keep rare path and config checks deferred unless a playbook truly needs them.
Use the live Starlight review page at http://10.167.2.150:18082/playbooks/asms-ui-is-down/ as the current operator review surface for the first subsystem-specific pack.
Use the saved lab target request and the prototype comparison ports 18083, 18084, and 18085 as the last reviewed prototype surfaces.

Use the generated site roots under dist/prototypes/starlight, dist/candidates/algosec-lab-baseline/starlight-site, and the current appliance-backed dist/candidates/adf-baseline/starlight-site when iterating on the Astro Starlight publisher.
stop_conditions: Stop when the current operator-review slice is captured cleanly and the next work items are recorded in pack-local state.
Stop and escalate if the next action would require changing registry, deployment pointer, or promotion state directly.
